body.title: channel.py post — reject unknown flags instead of silently ignoring
body.result: Shipped in d71bd3e6. _consume_flags helper rejects unknown -/-- args with clear error + exit 2. Applied to post, read, listen, sessions subcommands. --as value required (fails loudly if missing). 9 new tests in tests/test_channel_cli.py cover accept/reject/typo/missing-value paths across all four subcommands.
body.description:
channel.py post accepts --as <author> but silently ignores other flags (e.g. --author cc). The post still goes through with session-name as author, which Igor's ingestion filters may not recognize — message lands in channel but never reaches Igor's attention. Silent loss of signal; observed during T-gmail-login-page-object kickoff 2026-04-22.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/channel.py (the ad-hoc arg parser around line 223-230)
**Design rules:** none apply (tooling/ops shim).
**Scope boundary:** in — strict unknown-flag rejection for `post`. Touch the other subcommands (read, listen, sessions) while in the parser if the same pattern exists. Out — changing default author behavior; changing Igor's ingestion filter.
**Test plan:** unit test: `channel.py post 'msg' --bogus` exits non-zero with a clear error. Smoke: `--as claude-code` still works.
